poster_title: Generalist Reward Models: Found Inside Large Language Models
authors: Yi-Chen Li, Tian Xu, Yang Yu, Xuqin Zhang, Xiong-Hui Chen, Zhongxiang Ling, Ningjing Chao, Lei Yuan, Zhi-Hua Zhou

content: Aligning LLMs with human values is crucial in AI.
RLHF is effective but expensive and slow.
Alternative methods lack theoretical foundation and can be biased.
Building high-quality RMs is challenging and costly.
Existing methods for reward modeling are not scalable.
There is a need for a more efficient and principled approach.
Discovered a generalist reward model within LLMs.
The reward function can be recovered from next-token prediction.
Language model logits are interpreted as the soft Q-function.
Validated the theory with extensive experiments.
Used various LLMs to test the method.
Compared endogenous reward with LLM-as-a-judge and explicitly trained RMs.
Endogenous reward surpassed reward models trained on human-labeled data.
The method shows promise for efficient and scalable reward modeling.
Reward modeling can be replaced by eliciting knowledge from the base model.
This approach offers a more efficient and theoretically grounded paradigm.
Future work can explore the application in multi-modal models.
